steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The first sentence presents Ostrom's empirical finding: common pool resources can be sustainably managed by users. The second sentence then states that Ostrom's work repudiates (refutes) the “tragedy of the commons”—the theory claiming inevitable overexploitation. The key signal is that the second statement is a direct logical consequence of the first. Proving sustainable management is possible automatically contradicts a theory claiming it's impossible.

steps.title: Match the transition to the cause-effect relationship
steps.content:
“As such” means “consequently” or “therefore,” which directly captures how one statement follows logically from the other. Once Ostrom's evidence shows sustainable management is achievable, it necessarily follows that the tragedy of the commons—predicting inevitable failure—must be false. The other options all describe different relationships: “By contrast” would suggest two opposing views being presented separately; “For example” would treat the second statement as an illustration rather than a conclusion; “That said” would signal a concession or tangential remark, not a direct consequence.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
